examples.english: The predatory animal lives in the forest.

senses.gloss: verbal noun of حَيَّ (ḥayya): life; (especially) long, eternal life

examples.text: وَمَا هَٰذِهِ ٱلْحَيَاةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا إِلَّا لَهْوٌ وَلَعِبٌ وَإِنَّ ٱلدَّارَ ٱلْآخِرَةَ لَهِيَ ٱلْحَيَوَانُ لَوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ
examples.english: And this worldly life is not but diversion and amusement. And indeed, the home of the Hereafter – that is the [eternal] life, if only they knew.

etymology.text: Inherited from Proto-West Semitic *ḥayaw- (“animal”). Cognate with Hebrew חיה (ḥayyā), Classical Syriac ܚܝܘܬܐ (ḥaywəṯā) and Ugaritic 𐎈𐎆𐎉 (ḥwṭ). Synchronically, a verbal noun of حَيَّ (ḥayya, “to live”).
